title: The Supportiveness-Safety Tradeoff in LLM Well-Being Agents
abstract: 大型语言模型正被整合到社交辅助机器人及其他提供心理健康与福祉支持的情感对话系统中。这些智能体常被设计为表现出同理心与支持性，以提升用户参与度。然而，系统提示中支持性框架的增强如何影响安全相关行为，目前尚不明确。本研究通过80个涵盖四大福祉领域的合成问题，在三种支持程度不同的系统提示下评估了6个大型语言模型（共获得1440条回复）。采用经人工评分验证的模型评估框架，对回复的安全性与关怀质量进行测评。结果显示，适度支持性提示能在保障安全性的同时提升同理心与建设性支持；而过度肯定的提示则显著降低安全性，在某些情况下甚至损害所有领域的关怀质量，且不同模型间存在显著差异。本文进一步探讨了该发现对提示设计、模型选择及社交辅助机器人部署中领域特定安全机制的意义。
preview_text: Large language models (LLMs) are being integrated into socially assistive robots (SARs) and other conversational agents providing mental health and well-being support. These agents are often designed to sound empathic and supportive in order to maximize user's engagement, yet it remains unclear how increasing the level of supportive framing in system prompts influences safety relevant behavior. We evaluated 6 LLMs across 3 system prompts with varying levels of supportiveness on 80 synthetic queries spanning 4 well-being domains (1440 responses). An LLM judge framework, validated against human ratings, assessed safety and care quality. Moderately supportive prompts improved empathy and constructive support while maintaining safety. In contrast, strongly validating prompts significantly degraded safety and, in some cases, care across all domains, with substantial variation across models. We discuss implications for prompt design, model selection, and domain specific safeguards in SARs deployment.
one_line_summary: 该论文探讨LLM在心理健康支持代理中的支持性与安全性权衡，与给定关键词无关。
